codeReview.summary: The fix correctly addresses all three blocking issues from the prior review. GLOBAL_LLM_COST_TRACKER is now in swell-core and updated by both LLM backends (anthropic.rs, openai.rs) after successful chat() calls. DashboardState::sync_cost_from_global() polls the global tracker every 5 seconds and updates the dashboard cost state. EventEmitter::subscribe() enables real-time event broadcasting to WebSocket clients. OrchestratorEvent::AgentRegistered is subscribed in main.rs and forwarded to DashboardState::register_agent(). All blocking issues are resolved.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The Rust skill does not cover global tracker patterns using LazyLock + RwLock. The worker correctly implemented thread-safe global state but this pattern could be documented.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: cost_tracking.rs uses static GLOBAL_LLM_COST_TRACKER: LazyLock<std::sync::RwLock<GlobalLlmCostData>> - a specific Rust pattern that isn't mentioned in the skill file

fixAdequacy: adequate - The fix wires all three dead code paths from the prior review: (1) LLM backends now call record_llm_cost() after successful API calls, making DashboardState::update_cost() reachable via sync_cost_from_global(); (2) DashboardState::register_agent() is called from a tokio task in main.rs that subscribes to OrchestratorEvent::AgentRegistered; (3) EventEmitter now has subscribe() and broadcast infrastructure, and main.rs spawns a task that converts DaemonEvent to DashboardEvent and calls broadcast_event(). The cost_breakdown test passes. Clippy shows no warnings. Build succeeds.
